frozen_components: policy model during reward model training
architecture.summary: ER-PRM is a process reward model for multi-step LLM reasoning that scores individual intermediate steps rather than final outcomes. It derives reward values from KL-regularized MDPs, computing step rewards using the reference (initial) policy's completions via a soft-max formulation. The model is trained as an auto-regressive or regression-based classifier on step-level supervision, then applied at inference time for best-of-N selection or during RLHF rejection sampling to guide policy optimization.
trained_components: process reward model (Llama-3.1-8B, Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct, Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct base)
reward labeling function derived from entropy-regularized MDP

claims.statement: ER-PRM achieves 1% improvement on GSM8K and 2-3% improvement on MATH under best-of-N evaluation
ER-PRM achieves more than 1% improvement under RLHF
Entropy-regularized process reward model can be computed using the reference policy to generate completions, eliminating the need to learn reward and policy simultaneously
For Mistral-MetaMath-7B under RLHF rejection sampling, ER-PRM surpasses Soft-Label PRM by 0.8% on GSM8K and 2.7% on MATH
For DeepSeek-math-7B-instruct under RLHF rejection sampling, ER-PRM achieves 83.4±0.3% on GSM8K and 44.7±0.7% on MATH
3B reward model (Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct) on Mistral data achieves 41.9±1.0% best-of-64 accuracy on MATH500, approaching performance of 8B model
Regression-based training yields consistently lower best-of-N accuracies than auto-regressive training, with a significant gap on MATH
1B reward model (Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct) suffers from severe reward hacking on MATH, with accuracy dropping ~2% from best-of-16 to best-of-64
